title: Trajectory Artifact
description: Frozen Python<->TS contract: a SUMO run reduced to per-entity geographic trajectories, plus the scenario it represents, a sampled stakeholder-reaction layer, safety-surrogate conflicts, and a per-stakeholder scorecard. Positions are ALWAYS [lon, lat] (WGS84), never SUMO internal x/y. Do not change this schema without bumping schema_version and updating BOTH python/ and web/. v0.3.0 is ADDITIVE over v0.2.0: it adds optional persons[], conflicts[], scorecard, meta.scenario.change.target_lane, the agents grounding discriminator (sim|inferred), and optional agent person_id — all existing v0.2.0 artifacts remain valid.

allOf.$comment: v0.3.0 requires an explicit agent grounding. Older versions (0.1.0/0.2.0) predate it, so it is required only when schema_version is 0.3.0 — keeping the bump additive.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of THIS contract. 0.3.0 is current; 0.1.0 and 0.2.0 are accepted for back-compat reads (older artifacts simply omit the newer optional structures and the agent grounding field).

scenario.description: OPTIONAL (v0.2.0+). The proposed infrastructure change this run represents, vs. a baseline run. Absent for plain baseline runs.

baseline_run_id.description: run_id of the baseline (no-change) run this scenario is compared against.

type.description: Kind of infrastructure change. Intentionally a permissive enum; extend (additively) as new change types are modeled.

target_edge.description: SUMO edge id the change applies to.

target_lane.description: OPTIONAL (v0.3.0+). Lane index on target_edge the change applies to (e.g. the car lane converted to a bike lane). Omitted for edge-wide changes (e.g. speed_limit).

value_mps.description: OPTIONAL. Numeric parameter of the change in SI units (e.g. new speed limit in m/s). Omitted for changes with no scalar value (e.g. a new signal).

vehicles.description: Per-vehicle geographic trajectories. type is a free string; motorized traffic uses "car", cyclists use "bicycle" (v0.3.0+). Pedestrians are NOT here — they live in persons[].

path.description: Ordered [lon, lat] points (WGS84). Index-aligned with timestamps and speeds.

timestamps.description: Simulation time (s) for each path point. Index-aligned with path and speeds.

speeds.description: Speed (m/s) at each path point. Index-aligned with path and timestamps.

persons.description: OPTIONAL (v0.3.0+). Per-pedestrian geographic trajectories — SAME per-entity shape as vehicles[], but a distinct population (SUMO persons, not vehicles). type is a free string; pedestrians use "pedestrian".

path.description: Ordered [lon, lat] points (WGS84). Index-aligned with timestamps and speeds.

agents.description: OPTIONAL (v0.2.0+). Sampled stakeholder agents. NOT one per traveler — only a few sampled agents reason. A stakeholder-reaction PREVIEW, never a referendum or crash/ROI oracle. grounding (v0.3.0+) discriminates SIM-grounded agents (pinned to a simulated traveler via exactly one of vehicle_id/person_id, with a quantitative outcome + a playback trigger_t) from INFERRED agents (residents, businesses — no simulated trip, hence no pin/outcome/trigger_t). The sim/inferred field-presence invariant is enforced in the models, not this schema (schema stays loose to leave room).

grounding.description: REQUIRED for v0.3.0. "sim" = pinned to a simulated traveler (has one id + outcome + trigger_t); "inferred" = no simulated trip (no pin/outcome/trigger_t).

vehicle_id.description: OPTIONAL. id of the vehicle (in vehicles[]) a sim-grounded agent is pinned to. Mutually exclusive with person_id.

person_id.description: OPTIONAL (v0.3.0+). id of the person (in persons[]) a sim-grounded pedestrian agent is pinned to. Mutually exclusive with vehicle_id.

outcome.description: OPTIONAL. Quantitative baseline-vs-scenario outcome for a sim-grounded traveler (seconds). Absent for inferred agents.

reaction.description: The persona's anticipated reaction. A PREVIEW of texture/objection, not a verdict.

sentiment.description: Sentiment in [-1, 1]; -1 strongly negative, +1 strongly positive.

stance.description: Coarse stance toward the change. Intentionally a small permissive enum.

trigger_t.description: OPTIONAL. Simulation time (s) a sim-grounded agent's reaction is keyed to during playback. Absent for inferred agents.

conflicts.description: OPTIONAL (v0.3.0+). Safety-SURROGATE events computed from trajectories (time-to-collision, hard braking, blocked junctions) — NEVER a crash prediction. Deliberately under-constrained; 2.4 tightens.

severity.description: Surrogate magnitude. Uniform convention: higher = worse.

entities.description: OPTIONAL. ids (vehicles[]/persons[]) involved in the conflict.

scorecard.description: OPTIONAL (v0.3.0+). Per-STAKEHOLDER outcome summary — who wins, who loses, per group, on travel time / safety / access. NOT a single ROI. Deliberately under-constrained; 2.4 tightens.

grounding.description: "sim" = derived from simulated trips; "inferred" = no simulated trip for this group.

travel_time_delta.description: OPTIONAL & NULLABLE. Uniform sign: POSITIVE = WORSE for this group. null (or absent) = no signal / this group has no trip.

safety_delta.description: OPTIONAL & NULLABLE. POSITIVE = WORSE (less safe). null/absent = no signal.

access_delta.description: OPTIONAL & NULLABLE. POSITIVE = WORSE (less access). null/absent = no signal.

bca.description: OPTIONAL. Supplementary benefit-cost context. Deliberately unshaped (under-constrained) — 2.4 defines its structure.
